item.title: Nvidia Partner Hon Hai’s Sales Meet Estimates on Solid AI Demand
item.description: Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. reported a 29.7% rise in quarterly sales, a sign of sustained AI demand during the first weeks of war in the Middle East.

item.title: OPEC+ Plans Symbolic Oil Quota Hike Amid War, Delegates Say
item.description: OPEC+ members plan to raise their production quotas for May, in a symbolic move as the Middle East conflict constrains production and shipments from several of the alliance’s largest members.

item.title: Italy Sets Jet Fuel Limits at Some Airports on Supply Shortage
item.description: Several airports in Italy issued advisories of limited fuel supplies for the next few days as the conflict in the Middle East shows few signs of ending.

item.title: Danantara Advances Asset Manager Merger With $159 Million Deal
item.description: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund is advancing a plan to combine the asset management units of state-owned lenders to boost their regional competitiveness.

item.title: Missing US Airman Rescued as Iran Steps Up Attacks on Neighbors
item.description: US forces rescued an airman who went missing on Friday after Iran downed an American fighter jet in the country, President Donald Trump said.

item.title: Kuwaiti Oil Headquarters Set Ablaze by Iranian Drone Strike
item.description: Kuwait Petroleum Corp.’s headquarters caught fire after a strike by unmanned drones in the latest Iranian attack on its Persian Gulf neighbors.

item.title: Impulse Space, Anduril Building Space Technology for Golden Dome
item.description: Satellite startup Impulse Space is working with Anduril Industries Inc to develop space-based interceptor technology for President Donald Trump’s planned Golden Dome missile defense shield, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Inflation Seen Spiking in First Snapshot Since Iran War
item.description: The sudden increase in US gasoline prices felt by American consumers is set to be on full display in key inflation data due out this coming week.

item.title: Sleijpen Says ECB Discussion Will Be Rate Hike or Hold: Podcast
item.description: The European Central Bank’s next discussion will between increasing interest rates or holding them steady, according to Governing Council member Olaf Sleijpen.

item.title: Pakistan Says It Will Repay Matured Loan Deposits to UAE
item.description: Pakistan will return matured loan deposits to the United Arab Emirates, in a move that will strain the South Asian nation’s reserves.

item.title: IMF Warns Tokenized Finance Risks Amplifying Market Crises Ahead
item.description: Moving Wall Street’s trading infrastructure onto blockchain-based systems could accelerate financial crises beyond regulators’ ability to respond, even as the technology promises to cut costs and eliminate settlement delays, the International Monetary Fund says.

item.title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Weekly Transits Reach Highest Since War Began
item.description: Traffic through the vital Strait of Hormuz has been picking up in the past week, with the seven-day rolling average for transits on Friday reaching the highest since the war started.

item.title: Russia’s VPN Crackdown Caused Bank Outage, Telegram Founder Says
item.description: Russia’s attempts to restrict the use of virtual private networks amid a clampdown on the Telegram messaging platform triggered the widespread banking outage seen across the country this week, the service’s billionaire founder Pavel Durov said.

item.title: India Acknowledges Iranian Oil Purchases, Dismisses Payment Woes
item.description: India is buying crude from Iran among other countries in order to navigate the current energy crisis, the country’s oil ministry said, denying payment hurdles were impeding those purchases.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: Oil Storms Higher on War Escalation
item.description: Iran targeted more sites in Arab Gulf states overnight and into Friday, hours after US President Donald Trump issued fresh threats against Iranian infrastructure to pressure Tehran to start peace negotiations.

item.title: Russia Says Ship in Azov Sea Hit By Kyiv as Sides Swap Attacks
item.description: Russia said drone debris struck a foreign-flagged cargo vessel in a bay of the Azov Sea overnight while Moscow’s forces attacked several Ukrainian regions as the war showing no signs of slowing down.

item.title: Germany Among States Pushing EU to Tax Energy Windfall Profits
item.description: Five European Union members urged the bloc to tax windfall profits energy ​companies are reaping as a result of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: France to Offer Loans to Small Businesses Hit by Fuel Costs
item.description: The French government will offer loans of as much as €50,000 ($57,600) to small businesses that are the most exposed to rising fuel costs in transportation, fishing and agriculture.

item.title: Vietnam Growth Slows as Rising Energy Costs Feed Uncertainty
item.description: Vietnam’s economic momentum slowed in the first quarter as escalating tensions in the Middle East drive up energy costs and disrupt global trade routes, complicating General Secretary To Lam’s push for double-digit growth.

item.title: OpenAI COO Shifts Out of Role, AGI CEO Taking Medical Leave
item.description: OpenAI’s chief operating officer is shifting into a new role and two other top executives are going on leave due to health reasons, marking a major change to its executive bench ahead of a potential Wall Street debut as soon as this year.

item.title: US Judge Denies Pirro Motion to Revive Quashed Fed Subpoenas
item.description: The Justice Department lost its bid to continue an investigation into building-renovation cost overruns by the Federal Reserve, setting the stage for an appeal that could eventually reach the Supreme Court.

item.title: Bank of Italy Cuts Growth Forecast to 0.5% This Year and Next
item.description: The Bank of Italy lowered the country’s economic growth forecast for this year and next due to the impact of the US war on Iran.

item.title: It May Take a Year to Restore Abu Dhabi Aluminum Output, EGA Says
item.description: The biggest aluminum producer in the Middle East said it may take as long as a year to restore production of the metal at its facility in Abu Dhabi after the plant was damaged in an attack a week ago.

item.title: US Services Activity Contracts for First Time Since 2023
item.description: US services activity shrank in March for the first time in three years as a gauge of new business slumped in the wake of the Iran war.

item.title: Treasuries Fall as Strong Jobs Data Undermines Fed Cut Outlook
item.description: Treasuries fell as a solid reading of the US labor market prompted traders to reduce bets the Federal Reserve will lower interest rates this year.

item.title: Turkey Weighs Raising Energy Prices as Iran War Rattles Markets
item.description: Turkey is weighing whether to raise electricity and natural gas prices, according to people familiar with the matter, as surging global energy costs risk increasing the burden of state subsidies.

item.title: US Jobs Gain of 178,000 Tops Forecasts, Unemployment Falls
item.description: US job growth rebounded in March and the unemployment rate unexpectedly fell, suggesting the labor market was stabilizing as the Iran war began.

item.title: Jefferies Says UAE Injected $8 Billion Liquidity to Help Lenders
item.description: Analysts at Jefferies Financial Group Inc. estimate the United Arab Emirates’ central bank injected more than 30 billion dirhams ($8.2 billion) into the banking system to help protect against the impact of the Iran war.

item.title: Russia’s Oil Revenues Halved in March Before Iran War Boost
item.description: Russia’s oil tax revenue in March dropped by nearly half compared to a year earlier, highlighting the Kremlin’s financial strain just before the Middle East war delivered an unexpected boost to Moscow’s earnings.

item.title: Google Workers Find It’s a Different Era for Activism
item.description: Once at the forefront of the fight against military AI, employees now are on the sidelines
